dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3908 IS: SOS: Sustaining Outpatient Services Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Hoeven (for himself and Ms. Klobuchar) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to allow payments under the Medicare program for certain items and services furnished by off-campus outpatient departments of a provider to be determined under the prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department services, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the SOS: Sustaining Outpatient Services Act.

section.header: Allowing Medicare payments for certain items and services furnished by off-campus outpatient departments of a provider to be determined under the prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department services
section.text: Section 1833(t)(1)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395l(t)(1)(B)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (iv), by striking and at the end;

subparagraph.text: by inserting before does not include the following: subject to clause (vi),; and
by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.text: includes, with respect to a year (beginning with 2027), any item or service—

subclause.text: that is furnished during such year by an off-campus outpatient department of a provider (as defined in subparagraph (B) of paragraph (21)); and

with respect to which the greatest total amount paid with respect to a physician specialty under the physician fee schedule under section 1848 for all such items or services furnished by physicians in such specialty during the previous year was less than $2,000,000.
